meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve Deployment Errors
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging to crush deployment failures. This intermediate checklist uses Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures stem from environment mismatches, missing dependencies, or misconfigured CI/CD pipelines. By leveraging AI agents like Claude Code to audit your logs and Antigravity to simulate production environments, you can isolate root causes in seconds, transform cryptic stack traces into actionable fixes, and ship your production-ready code confidently.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the build progress bar hits 99%, and then—the dreaded red text. Your deployment has crashed, and your production environment is in shambles. For a student builder, this moment is the ultimate crucible; it is the wall between a hobby project and a professional-grade SaaS application. You are not just fighting code; you are fighting the ambiguity of infrastructure.

But the era of manual log-diving is over. With modern AI-native tools, you can treat your deployment pipeline as an intelligent system that explains its own failures. By integrating Claude Code and Antigravity into your workflow, you move from guessing why a build failed to executing surgical precision fixes that get your app live on the web.

steps.title: Analyze Deployment Logs with Claude
steps.description: Feed your raw deployment logs into Claude Code to identify the specific exit code causing the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following deployment log output. Identify the root cause, explain why the environment failed to build, and suggest the exact configuration change needed in my environment variables or dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /read logs/deploy.log 'Identify the specific error causing this deployment failure and draft a fix.'

steps.title: Simulate Production Environment
steps.description: Use Antigravity to mirror your production environment variables locally to ensure your build script behaves predictably.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a local container configuration that replicates my production build environment, including node version, OS dependencies, and secret injection.

steps.title: Audit Dependency Tree
steps.description: Deployment often fails due to 'dependency hell'. Use Claude Code to audit your package.json against your target platform's limitations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my current package.json and lockfile. Identify any incompatible versions or packages that require native builds not supported by my hosting provider.

steps.title: Automate CI/CD Pipeline Fixes
steps.description: Refactor your CI/CD yaml configuration using AI-driven best practices to prevent future build-time regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my deployment yaml script to include better error handling, caching for node_modules, and a pre-flight check for environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit .github/workflows/deploy.yml 'Optimize for build speed and add pre-flight validation.'

steps.title: Execute Final Deployment Verification
steps.description: Perform a dry-run deployment to validate that the changes have resolved the blocking issue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Verify the fix by running a simulated deployment process. Report any remaining warnings before I trigger the final push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run --dry-run 'npm run build && npm run deploy'

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file to force AI agents to recognize missing environment keys before they reach the build server.
When debugging, ask the AI to explain the error using the 'Feynman Technique' to ensure you understand the infrastructure bottleneck.
Use Antigravity to snapshot your environment state before applying any AI-suggested patches to ensure easy rollbacks.
If your build fails on a specific platform like Vercel or Railway, provide the AI with the specific build-image documentation link to contextualize constraints.
Treat your deployment scripts as first-class code; use Claude Code to write unit tests for your CI/CD logic itself.
content.next_steps: Once your build is green, move on to setting up automated monitoring and error logging to ensure you are the first to know if something goes wrong in production.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools to conquer the deployment gap. Stop letting infrastructure errors stall your momentum and start shipping products that scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
